subject: [PATCH] mm/mempolicy: Fix weighted interleave auto sysfs name

reviews.author: Rakie Kim
reviews.summary: Reviewer Rakie Kim acknowledged that Joshua Hahn's patch was a good catch to resolve an unexpected global macro expansion issue, and approved the fix.
reviews.raw_body:
Hello Joshua,

This was indeed an unfortunate and tricky issue to track down, given
that it was caused by an unexpected global macro expansion. great catch,
and thanks for the quick response and clean fix.

Reviewed-by: Rakie Kim <rakie.kim@sk.com>

reviews.author: Zi Yan
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch description does not match the actual changes made to the code, specifically pointing out that the original commit message mentioned using __ATTR macro but the current patch bypasses VERIFY_OCTAL_PERMISSIONS check.
reviews.raw_body:
Acked-by: Zi Yan <ziy@nvidia.com>

Best Regards,
Yan, Zi

2026-04-08.patch_summary: This patch fixes a naming issue in the weighted interleave auto sysfs attribute by directly passing a string 'auto' instead of the raw token, which was being expanded to __auto_type. This change prevents the sysfs entry from displaying as __auto_type and ensures it shows as 'auto'. The patch also bypasses an unnecessary permissions check for this specific file. The fix is based on a recent commit in the mm-new branch.

reviews.author: Huang, Ying
reviews.summary: Reviewer requested that Fixes tag be added to the commit message for easier backporting and suggested adding Acked-by: Huang Ying in future versions
reviews.raw_body:
Fixes tag helps people to decide whether to backport the commit.  So, I
think that we need it anyway.  Feel free to add

Acked-by: Huang Ying <ying.huang@linux.alibaba.com>

in the future versions.

[snip]

---
Best Regards,
Huang, Ying

2026-04-09.patch_summary: This patch fixes a naming issue in the weighted interleave auto sysfs attribute by directly passing a string 'auto' instead of the raw token, which was being expanded to __auto_type. This change prevents the sysfs entry from displaying as __auto_type and ensures it shows as 'auto'. The patch also bypasses an unnecessary permissions check for this specific file. The fix is based on a recent commit in the mm-new branch.
